settings.title: Get Tax Year End Ready With Us
settings.excerpt: An early-evening session in March to help self-employed clients close the year without the last-minute panic.
settings.body:
The end of the tax year tends to arrive in a rush, and a rushed return is where mistakes creep in. A little preparation now saves a great deal of stress later.

On the evening of 19 March, Ledgerworks is hosting a practical session for self-employed clients. We will walk through a simple checklist of what to gather, what to set aside, and the deadlines worth marking now.

What the checklist covers

The session is built around the things people most often leave until the last minute. We go through them in plain terms, with time to ask about your own situation rather than just listen to general advice.

  • The records and receipts worth gathering now
  • How much to set aside so the bill is no surprise
  • The deadlines that are genuinely worth marking

None of it is complicated once it is laid out in order. The value is in doing it calmly in March rather than in a panic when the deadline is days away.

What to bring

It runs for an hour with tea and questions afterward. Come with your records as messy as they are. Sorting them out together is exactly the point.

You do not need to tidy anything up beforehand or feel embarrassed about the state of your paperwork. We have seen every kind of filing system and every kind of carrier bag. The whole reason for the evening is to turn that pile into something manageable while there is still time to do it well.
